economic_notes: First Nation operates fisheries including DFO operations and fish hatchery farm, Rod's One Stop retail, Waycobah Contracting Incorporated (security company), trout farm, fitness centre, and gaming from Province of Nova Scotia casino profits. Member of E'sukutimkewey Limited Partnership with 12 other First Nation entities. Purchased fishing licenses totaling $15,175,000. First Nations Finance Authority debt totaling over $54 million.
notes: Consolidated entities include We'koqma'q One Stop, We'koqma'q Housing Projects, and We'koqma'q Contracting Incorporated. Net debt position of $39.7M. Significant long-term debt of $73M including large FNFA loans. Purchased fishing licenses of $15.175M (intangible assets, not amortized). Promissory note receivable of $24.25M from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership. ISC-specific funding not broken out separately in the government transfers note — Note 18 referenced but table not included in provided text. Tangible capital assets schedule not provided in extracted text. No schedule of remuneration included in provided text. CMHC replacement reserve deficiency of $631,133. Adopted PS 3400 Revenue standard April 1, 2023.
